---
title: "Sofia Sarak"
image: images/sarak_headshot.jpg
about:
  template: trestles
  image-shape: rounded
  #image-width: 
  links:
    - text: LinkedIn
      icon: linkedin
      href: https://linkedin.com/in/sofiasarak
      target: _blank # ensures that link is opened in a new browser tab
    - text: GitHub
      icon: github
      href: https://github.com/sofiasarak
      target: -blank
    - text: Email
      icon: envelope
      href: mailto:sarak@ucsb.edu
---

<h1>Welcome!</h1>

My name is Sofia, and I am an early-career data scientist currently completing a Master of Environmental Data Science at the Bren School of Environmental Science & Management at UCSB (whew, that was a mouthful). I am still finding my niche, but I am currently enjoying learning data visualization methods, geospatial analysis, and environmental policy evaluation.

<h3>About Me</h3>

I grew up in southern Connecticut, and ended up at Wesleyan University for my undergraduate education. I double-majored in Earth & Environmnetal Sciences and Economics, and am continuously surprised at how interconnected the two fields are. 

Some of my past projects include exploring evolutionary divergence of fish species, trophic levels of ants in Puerto Rico, and testing for bacteria concentrations at a water quality monitoring organization.

In my free time, I enjoy hiking, swimming, reading, and doing little crafts like crocheting and sewing. I'm also a big fan of the Great British Baking Show and Bad Bunny. Current personal projects include practicing my Spanish (I traveled to Argentina this winter and was able to get around!) and potentially running another half marathon soon.
